# stalwart_network_listener (Resource)

Manages a Stalwart network listener (the `NetworkListener` JMAP object). A listener binds to one or more addresses and handles a specific mail or HTTP protocol.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "stalwart_network_listener" "smtp" {
  name     = "smtp"
  bind     = ["0.0.0.0:25"]
  protocol = "smtp"
}

resource "stalwart_network_listener" "imaps" {
  name         = "imaps"
  bind         = ["0.0.0.0:993"]
  protocol     = "imap"
  tls_implicit = true
}

# Import an existing listener by its name or opaque id:
# terraform import stalwart_network_listener.smtp smtp
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bind` (Set of String) Set of `host:port` addresses the listener binds to, e.g. `["0.0.0.0:25"]`.
- `name` (String) Unique name for this listener, e.g. `smtp`, `smtps`, `imaps`. Changing the name replaces the listener.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol handled by this listener: `smtp`, `lmtp`, `imap`, `pop3`, `http`, or `manageSieve`.

### Optional

- `override_proxy_trusted_networks` (Set of String) CIDR blocks trusted to send proxy-protocol headers for this listener, e.g. `["10.10.10.0/24"]`. Overrides the global proxy trusted network list.
- `tls_implicit` (Boolean) Whether TLS is negotiated immediately on connection (implicit TLS / SMTPS/IMAPS). Set to `true` for port 465 (SMTPS) and port 993 (IMAPS). Defaults to `false`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Opaque server-assigned identifier of the listener.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by listener name (preferred):
terraform import stalwart_network_listener.smtp smtp

# Import by opaque server-assigned id:
# terraform import stalwart_network_listener.smtp <opaque-id>
```
